description_long: This object consists of a cluster of three mushrooms with broad, rounded caps that are dark brown and covered in numerous small, pale beige spots. The caps are slightly domed and have a matte, somewhat rough appearance, with the largest mushroom positioned at the back and two smaller ones in front. Each mushroom has a thick, cylindrical stem that is darker brown at the top and transitions to a lighter, almost white color near the base, suggesting a natural gradient. The stems are robust and slightly faceted, giving them a stylized, polygonal look. The gills beneath the caps are not visible from any angle, and the mushrooms appear to be growing closely together as a group. The overall form and coloration evoke the appearance of wild mushrooms commonly found in parks or natural urban green spaces.
description: A cluster of three brown mushrooms with spotted caps and thick, gradient-colored stems, grouped closely together. The caps are domed and covered in pale spots, while the stems are sturdy and transition from dark to light brown.
description_view_0: Three mushrooms are visible, with the largest cap on the left and two smaller mushrooms to the right, showing the spotted pattern and gradient stems.
description_view_1: The cluster is seen more head-on, with all three caps clearly visible and the largest mushroom centered at the back.
description_view_2: The largest mushroom is on the right, with the two smaller mushrooms to the left, highlighting the cap shapes and stem gradients.
description_view_3: The view is focused on the largest mushroom cap in the foreground, with the two smaller mushrooms partially visible behind.
